notes:
Opening opportunity work notes

Objective: plaintiff opening for arb-ex06-20260630015257. Proposition resolves YES if U.S. ground forces entered Venezuelan sovereign territory in combat capacity with objective of seizing/holding territory, removing sitting government, or establishing operational control of a major population center; official DoD/White House statements primary, UNSC records fallback; expiration Jan. 31, 2026.

Record/evidence scan: wait_for_opportunity returned openings:plaintiff ready; get_case shows empty record (no evidence, exhibits, submitted_evidence, arguments, openings, technical_reports); list_evidence returned empty. No visible case-packet evidence to stat/read.

Source map: decisive proof should come from official White House or DoD/War.gov statements confirming U.S. Armed Forces operation, land/ground component, entry into Venezuelan territory/Caracas, combat capacity, and objective. Secondary/fallback source target: UN Security Council meeting record S/PV.10085 and related Security Council records.

Investigation performed before opening: web searches targeted DoD/White House/UN sources. Found likely later evidence targets: White House Jan. 4 release with Secretary Rubio quotes (including that U.S. forces were on the ground in Venezuela for about two hours to capture Maduro, plus adverse language that it was not invasion/extended operation); White House Jan. 9 fact sheet saying U.S. armed forces conducted an extraordinary military operation in support of law enforcement to bring Maduro to justice; War.gov/DoD-style article titled 'Trump Announces U.S. Military's Capture of Maduro' describing a U.S. armed forces operation in capital/heart of Caracas, Operation Absolute Resolve; UN Digital Library S/PV.10085 PDF; Security Council Report Feb. 2026 PDF discussing Jan. 3 U.S. operation in Venezuela and Jan. 5 emergency meeting. Source URLs and search log saved in /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Adverse checks: strongest defense is the event's edge case: capture of a head of state without territorial occupation is NO. White House/Rubio language also says no current U.S. ground forces, only about two hours on ground, not a war, not an invasion, not an extended military operation. Plaintiff needs later admitted evidence to show the operation satisfies the 'removing the sitting government' objective and/or operational control language, not merely a standalone capture raid. Need careful evidence admission in argument phase; opening should not cite unadmitted source text as record proof.

Filing decision: record is empty, so opening will be forward-looking: state plaintiff theory, identify decisive factual questions, explain expected official-source proof, candidly identify the NO edge-case/adverse theory, and reserve actual source reliance until evidence can be admitted.

Defendant opening opportunity openings:defendant. Record/evidence check: get_case showed only plaintiff opening; no arguments/exhibits/submitted evidence/technical reports. list_evidence returned empty evidence list, so no case-packet files or visible evidence to stat/read. Opening phase allows only record_opening_statement; evidence submission unavailable.

Issue breakdown: Proposition resolves YES only if U.S. ground forces entered Venezuelan sovereign territory in combat capacity with objective of seizing/holding territory, removing sitting government, or establishing operational control of major population center. Express exclusions/edge cases: targeted strikes, naval blockades, special operations raids without territorial objectives, cyber operations; capture of head of state without territorial occupation is NO.

Investigation/source map: searched official White House, Defense/War.gov, and UN press records. Likely adverse plaintiff source target: DOW/War.gov Jan. 3, 2026 article titled 'Trump Announces U.S. Military's Capture of Maduro' describing Operation Absolute Resolve in Caracas, ground-based extraction force, Maduro out of power, and some U.S. oversight/transition language. Likely defense-favorable official target: White House Jan. 4, 2026 Rubio release stating no current U.S. ground forces in Venezuela, troops were there about two hours to capture Maduro, not war against Venezuela, not an invasion, not an extended military operation, and retained options/pressure. UN SC/16271 appears likely relevant fallback/adverse source, but the web open had an internal error after search result; retry in evidence phase if conflict/fallback matters.

Searches run: site:whitehouse.gov Venezuela U.S. ground forces entered Venezuela January 2026 Maduro operation; site:defense.gov Venezuela U.S. ground forces entered Venezuela operation January 2026; site:whitehouse.gov Venezuela Maduro capture U.S. forces operation 2026; site:press.un.org Security Council Venezuela United States military operation ground forces January 2026. Opened official DOW/War.gov and White House pages. No sources submitted because current phase disallows evidence submission.

Reasoning: Defense should not deny that likely official sources may show a brief U.S. ground entry/capture in Caracas. The strongest legal defense is that plaintiff must prove the qualifying objective, and the proposition expressly treats capture of a head of state without territorial occupation as NO. If admitted official records show a short extraction/capture raid and pressure/transition aftermath, that is not enough unless it also proves territorial seizure/holding, operational control of a major population center, or a removal objective within the proposition rather than mere law-enforcement capture/outcome. Opening must avoid citing unadmitted source content as record proof, but can identify source targets and burden gaps.

Durable journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with work log, source URLs, gaps, and later evidence plan.

Merits opportunity notes

Opportunity: arguments:plaintiff. Rechecked current record and evidence list before filing. get_case showed plaintiff/defendant openings only; no prior evidence, submitted evidence, technical reports, or arguments. list_evidence empty before submissions.

Evidence plan/source map: primary official sources needed to prove U.S. Armed Forces; ground entry into Venezuela; combat capacity; qualifying objective by Jan. 31, 2026. Strongest source target was official Defense/War Department page; confirming/limiting White House sources; UN Security Council record as fallback/secondary.

Evidence submitted and stat-checked:
1. ev_f28c5a402415_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-f28c5a402415, sha256 f28c5a402415eaff430e99d5b13d7695ff7d5cc29eb334758cff324f2b138b92. War.gov/Defense Department News article. Official .gov; retrieved 2026-06-30. Proves Jan. 3, 2026 U.S. military operation in Caracas, Venezuela; joint military extraction; Trump statement that U.S. armed forces conducted an extraordinary military operation in Venezuela's capital/heart of Caracas; special operations forces; air/ground/space/maritime operations; more than 150 aircraft covering ground-based extraction force in Caracas; Maduro out of power; U.S. to oversee/run Venezuela until safe transition.
2. ev_f9ff71d245a2_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-f9ff71d245a2, sha256 f9ff71d245a2af214fee4ccafc9081674c5b0ad0899c7cdce17c3aa541556beb. Official White House Rubio release. Proves U.S. forces were on the ground in Venezuela for about two hours to capture Maduro. Adverse/limiting language: no current ground forces, not war against Venezuela, not invasion, not extended military operation.
3. ev_b6e00c4389f6_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-b6e00c4389f6, sha256 b6e00c4389f6ad59750ce430dfcad786ad33c9b5378ee14bf366579aafb170cf. UN Security Council S/PV.10085 identifying meeting record for Jan. 5, 2026. Secondary/fallback context; not main proof.
4. ev_ade5f873c616_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-ade5f873c616, sha256 ade5f873c616306332f6c1213842f9578f221a5fb98f47dd38c879aa70c5475b. White House Jan. 9 fact sheet. Confirms U.S. armed forces conducted extraordinary operation in support of law enforcement to bring Maduro to justice; adverse/limiting law-enforcement support phrasing.

Adverse checks: Defendant's best point is edge case: capture of head of state without territorial occupation = NO. White House/Rubio says ground presence lasted about two hours and was not invasion/extended operation. My planned response: proposition has alternative qualifying objectives; official Defense/War source does more than describe a capture, because it says Maduro was out of power and Trump said U.S. would oversee/running Venezuela until transition, while the ground-based extraction force operated in Caracas under military cover. That supports objective of removing sitting government, not merely transient custody. Still acknowledge no proof of long-term territorial occupation; argument rests on removal objective.

Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with source URLs, evidence IDs, hashes, custody notes, theory, weaknesses, and unresolved gaps. No packages installed; no browser beyond web.open; no OCR needed because submitted extractions are text-readable.

Defendant merits opportunity arguments:defendant. Record check completed with get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff submitted four visible text evidence items and exhibits: PX-1 War.gov article (ev_f28c5a402415..., sha256 f28c5a402415eaff430e99d5b13d7695ff7d5cc29eb334758cff324f2b138b92, 3803 bytes), PX-2 White House Rubio release (ev_f9ff71d245a2..., sha256 f9ff71d245a2af214fee4ccafc9081674c5b0ad0899c7cdce17c3aa541556beb, 2354 bytes), PX-3 UN Security Council record (ev_b6e00c4389f6..., sha256 b6e00c4389f6ad59750ce430dfcad786ad33c9b5378ee14bf366579aafb170cf, 1399 bytes), PX-4 White House fact sheet (ev_ade5f873c616..., sha256 ade5f873c616306332f6c1213842f9578f221a5fb98f47dd38c879aa70c5475b, 1323 bytes). Used stat_evidence and read_evidence_range on all four.

Evidence assessment: PX-1 and PX-2 prove U.S. ground presence in Caracas/Venezuela and a military/special operations extraction/raid; contest is not physical entry. PX-2 and PX-4 contain key limits: no current U.S. ground forces, ground presence about two hours, not war against Venezuela, not invasion, not extended military operation, operation in support of law enforcement to bring Maduro to justice. PX-3 mostly identifies a UN record and is submitted for fallback/provenance; no substantive excerpt resolving objective. PX-1 has adverse language: Maduro out of power, U.S. to oversee Venezuela until transition, group running Venezuela, Rodriguez willing to work with U.S.; defense response is that these are post-capture political/transition statements and do not prove the ground force's qualifying objective or territorial/operational control. If capture + political consequences automatically satisfied removal, the explicit edge case 'capture of a head of state without territorial occupation: NO' would be meaningless.

Additional investigation/evidence: Opened White House Jan. 5 release 'Democrats Once Demanded Maduro's Ouster. Now They Mourn His Capture -- Because Trump Did It.' Submitted excerpt as defense evidence, accepted as ev_78e9314f1e93_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-78e9314f1e93, sha256 78e9314f1e93d7d3d0ff5029b31289764b53f2bd1af4eb01716e8f35a57cce59, 1952 bytes. It characterizes the event as 'capture and extradition' with Maduro detained on U.S. soil to face justice, supporting law-enforcement/custody framing. Private journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Planned argument: concede brief ground entry/combat-capable raid; argue plaintiff fails the qualifying-objective element. Offer PX-1, PX-2, PX-4, and DX-1 (defense evidence). Emphasize official White House limits and the proposition's express edge case/exclusion for capture raids without territorial objectives. No need for further outside search before filing because admitted official record contains both the strongest plaintiff and defense materials; the dispute is legal weight/inference, not missing source class.

Rebuttal opportunity notes

Opportunity: rebuttals:plaintiff. Rechecked current record in prompt; defendant filed merits argument and submitted one new evidence item. Scanned list_evidence: five evidence items now visible, including defendant ev_78e9314f1e93_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-78e9314f1e93. Stat checked defendant item: text/plain, 1952 bytes, juror-visible, sha256 78e9314f1e93d7d3d0ff5029b31289764b53f2bd1af4eb01716e8f35a57cce59, official White House release URL, retrieved 2026-06-30T02:04:00Z. read_evidence_range offset 0 length 4096 returned the full item.

Defendant's strongest point: concedes U.S. ground entry in Caracas, military/law-enforcement raid or extraction, and Maduro capture, but argues edge case/special-ops exclusion controls because no territorial occupation or operational control indicators and official statements call it capture/extradition/arrest/law-enforcement support.

Defendant exhibit analysis: The White House release title is 'Democrats Once Demanded Maduro's Ouster. Now They Mourn His Capture -- Because Trump Did It.' It states Trump achieved 'capture and extradition' and Maduro is detained on U.S. soil. It also says Democrats wanted Maduro 'delegitimized, pressured, and removed' and preserves quotes calling the action an invasion and military attack to arrest Maduro. It does not retract PX-1's War.gov statements that Maduro was out of power and U.S. would oversee/run Venezuela until transition.

Rebuttal plan: targeted answer only. Emphasize defendant conceded most elements; question is qualifying objective. Edge case says capture without territorial occupation, but the record has more: PX-1 ground-based extraction force in Caracas plus Maduro out of power and U.S. running/overseeing Venezuela pending transition. Duration and law-enforcement labels do not defeat alternative objective of removing sitting government. No new evidence needed; use admitted evidence and defendant evidence. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant surrebuttal opportunity surrebuttals:defendant. Record checked with get_case/list_evidence. No new evidence submitted by plaintiff in rebuttal; plaintiff reused/stat-read defendant evidence ev_78e9314f1e93 as PX-R1. Existing evidence list unchanged: PX-1 War.gov article, PX-2 White House Rubio release, PX-3 UN Security Council record, PX-4 White House fact sheet, DX-1 White House capture/extradition release.

Plaintiff rebuttal's strongest point: defense allegedly reads edge case too broadly because 'removing the sitting government' is an alternative to occupation, and PX-1's post-operation statements about Maduro out of power plus U.S. transition oversight show a qualifying removal objective.

Defense response: the specific edge case 'capture of a head of state without territorial occupation: NO' must have effect. Where plaintiff's claimed removal is the capture itself plus political aftermath, the specific carve-out controls over the general removal language. Otherwise any capture of a political leader would always become YES if it produces political change. Evidence response: PX-1's operational language is capture/extraction/bring to justice; PX-2/PX-4/DX-1 official White House sources characterize the event as raid/capture/arrest/law enforcement/extradition, two hours, not invasion, not extended operation. Post-capture 'oversee/running' language is ambiguous political aftermath, not proof that the ground force's objective was territorial control or a qualifying non-carveout removal operation. No further source search or evidence submission needed; dispute is interpretation of existing official record. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Closing opportunity notes

Rechecked record with get_case and list_evidence before filing. Record now includes plaintiff and defendant openings, arguments, plaintiff rebuttal, defendant surrebuttal, five evidence items, and technical reports. Evidence list shows five text-readable items: plaintiff PX-1 War.gov/Defense source, PX-2 White House Rubio release, PX-3 UN Security Council record, PX-4 White House fact sheet, and defendant DX-1 White House capture/extradition release. No new case-packet files or changed metadata observed beyond the already-known defendant exhibit.

Defendant surrebuttal: argues edge case controls; says plaintiff's theory is capture plus political aftermath; says PX-1/PX-2/PX-4/DX-1 state capture, arrest, extradition, prosecution; says transition language appears after capture and does not prove ground troops entered with governmental replacement objective; says no territorial occupation.

Closing plan: Use only existing record. Do not add evidence or reports. Emphasize conceded facts: U.S. ground entry in Caracas, military/law-enforcement raid/extraction, capture. Focus the remaining issue: whether official record proves qualifying objective. Weight PX-1 as strongest official Defense/War source: Jan. 3, 2026 military operation in Caracas; ground-based extraction force; special ops and integrated air/ground/space/maritime; Maduro out of power; U.S. to oversee/run Venezuela pending transition. Acknowledge adverse PX-2/PX-4/DX-1 labels and two-hour/no-invasion/law-enforcement limitations. Argue those defeat long-term occupation but not the alternative objective of removing the sitting government. Edge case cannot erase explicit alternative when official source goes beyond capture to removal and U.S.-directed transition. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant closing opportunity closings:defendant. Closing-phase get_case and list_evidence completed. Evidence list unchanged: PX-1 War.gov article ev_f28c5a402415, PX-2 White House Rubio ev_f9ff71d245a2, PX-3 UN record ev_b6e00c4389f6, PX-4 White House fact sheet ev_ade5f873c616, DX-1 White House capture/extradition ev_78e9314f1e93. No new evidence or metadata changes requiring additional reads; exact relevant contents were already read in merits and addressed again in surrebuttal.

Plaintiff closing repeats key conceded facts (ground entry in Caracas, military/combat capacity) and argues the official record proves the alternative objective of removing the sitting government via PX-1's 'out of power'/'oversee'/'running Venezuela' transition language. Defense closing plan: concede physical entry and combat-capable raid; focus entirely on unresolved qualifying objective. Specific edge case 'capture of head of state without territorial occupation: NO' must be given effect. PX-1's own operational language is capture/extraction/bring to justice; PX-2/PX-4/DX-1 narrow the event to two-hour, capture/arrest, law-enforcement, extradition, no invasion, no extended operation. The record lacks full transcript and occupation/control indicators; transition language is at most political aftermath/inference. Preponderance not met on objective distinct from capture. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
